example: A paroxysm of coughing interrupted the speaker in the middle of his address.
mnemonic: par(넘어서) + oxysm(날카로운, 그리스어 oxys=날카로운) = 날카롭게 치솟는 → 발작. oxy(날카로운): oxygen(산을 만드는), oxide

example: Her calm expression belied the anxiety she felt inside.
mnemonic: be(완전히) + lie(거짓) = 거짓을 드러내다. 겉모습이 내면과 모순되다

example: The dearth of qualified teachers has become a national crisis.
mnemonic: dear(비싼, 고대 영어 dyre) + th = 비싸서 부족한 → 결핍. dear(비싼): 공급이 적으면 값이 비싸진다

example: The unexpected question discomfited the speaker during the press conference.
mnemonic: dis(부정) + comfit(만들다, 준비하다) = 준비를 무너뜨리다 → 당황하게 하다. confit(만들다): confection

example: The violence revealed an atavistic instinct buried deep within human nature.
mnemonic: 라틴어 atavus(고조부). 먼 조상의 특성이 되돌아온 → 격세유전의, 원시적인

example: The peripatetic musician performed in different cities every week.
mnemonic: peri(주위를) + patet(걷다, 그리스어 patein) + ic = 주위를 걸어다니는 → 떠돌아다니는. 아리스토텔레스가 걸으며 가르친 학파

example: The critic described the movie as insipid and lacking any originality.
mnemonic: '인시피드' → 'in(없는)+sipid(맛)' 맛이 없는 따분한!

example: The sibilant whisper of the wind through the reeds created an eerie atmosphere.
mnemonic: sibil(쉬쉬 소리, 라틴어 sibilare=휘파람 불다) + ant = 쉬쉬 소리 나는. 's', 'sh' 같은 치찰음을 묘사하는 단어

example: The speaker stepped up to the rostrum and addressed the crowd.
mnemonic: 라틴어 rostrum(부리, 배의 뱃머리). 로마에서 전리품 뱃머리로 장식한 연단 → 연단

example: Her perspicacity in business matters made her an invaluable advisor.
mnemonic: per(통하여) + spic(보다, 라틴어 specere) + acity = 꿰뚫어 보는 능력 → 통찰력. perspicacious(통찰력 있는)의 명사형

example: The criminals who perpetrated the robbery were caught within hours.
mnemonic: per(완전히) + petr(행하다, 라틴어 patrare) + ate = 완전히 행하다 → (범죄를) 저지르다. perpetrator(범인)

example: The glib salesman convinced many customers to buy unnecessary products.
mnemonic: 중세 저지 독일어 glibberich(미끄러운)에서 유래. 말이 미끄럽듯 잘 나오는 → 말 잘하는(부정적)

example: The scholar provided a detailed exegesis of the ancient religious text.
mnemonic: ex(밖으로) + ege(이끌다, 그리스어 hegeisthai) + sis = 의미를 밖으로 이끌어내다 → 주석, 해석

example: The general's perfidy in switching sides shocked his former allies.
mnemonic: per(완전히, 나쁘게) + fid(믿음, 라틴어 fides) + y = 믿음을 완전히 저버림 → 배신. fid(믿음): fidelity, confide, infidel, bona fide

example: The witness gave a mendacious account of what happened that night.
mnemonic: mend(거짓, 라틴어 mendax) + acious = 거짓말하는. mend 어근은 '결함'을 의미

example: The supercilious waiter looked down on customers who ordered cheap wine.
mnemonic: super(위에) + cili(눈썹, 라틴어 cilium) + ous = 눈썹을 치켜올린 → 거만한. 눈썹을 올리며 내려다보는 표정

example: The retirement speech turned into a panegyric for the departing CEO.
mnemonic: pan(모든) + egyr(모임, 그리스어 agyris) + ic = 모든 사람 앞에서의 찬양 → 찬양 연설

example: The student's recalcitrance in following rules led to his suspension.
mnemonic: re(뒤로) + calcit(발로 차다, 라틴어 calcitrare) + ance = 뒤로 발차기하는 → 반항. calc(발): recalcitrant

example: High recidivism rates suggest that the prison system needs reform.
mnemonic: re(다시) + cid(떨어지다, 라틴어 cadere) + ivism = 다시 떨어지는 것 → 재범. cad(떨어지다): cadence, decay, incident

example: The charity provides food and shelter for destitute families.
mnemonic: de(떨어져) + stitute(세우다, 라틴어 statuere) = 세워진 것에서 떨어진 → 궁핍한. stitu(세우다): institute, constitute, substitute

example: The bombing caused indiscriminate destruction of civilian homes.
mnemonic: in(아닌) + discriminate(구별하다) = 구별하지 않는 → 무차별의. crim/cern(구별): discriminate, discern

example: The exigent circumstances required immediate intervention by the government.
mnemonic: ex(밖으로) + ig(구하다, 라틴어 agere) + ent = 밖으로 구하는 → 긴급한. exig(요구): exigency, exact(정확히 요구하다)

example: She reproached him for not keeping his promise.
mnemonic: re(되돌려) + proach(가까이, 라틴어 prope) = 가까이 되돌아와 → 비난하다. proach(가까이): approach

example: The president issued an unequivocal condemnation of the attack.
mnemonic: un(아닌) + equivocal(모호한) = 모호하지 않은 → 명백한. equi(같은)+voc(목소리): 양쪽 목소리가 같지 않은

example: She stared at him with incredulity when he claimed to have won the lottery.
mnemonic: in(아닌) + cred(믿다) + ulity = 믿지 않음 → 불신. cred(믿다): credit, incredible, credulous, creed

example: The principle of parsimony suggests choosing the simplest explanation.
mnemonic: parsimoni(절약, 라틴어 parcere=아끼다) + y = 극도로 아끼는 → 인색. parsimonious(인색한)

example: The prisoner languished in jail for years awaiting trial.
mnemonic: langu(축 처지다, 라틴어 languere) + ish = 축 처져 시들다. languid(축 처진)와 같은 어근

example: The protracted negotiations finally ended after eighteen months.
mnemonic: pro(앞으로) + tract(끌다, 라틴어 trahere) + ed = 앞으로 끌어당긴 → 오래 끈. tract(끌다): attract, extract, contract

example: Hackers found a way to circumvent the company's security system.
mnemonic: circum(둘레) + vent(오다, 라틴어 venire) = 둘레를 돌아오다 → 우회하다. ven(오다): advent, convention, prevent

example: The convoluted plot of the novel confused many readers.
mnemonic: con(함께) + volut(감다, 라틴어 volvere) + ed = 함께 감긴 → 뒤얽힌, 복잡한. volv(감다): revolve, evolve, involve

example: Under pressure, the witness recanted his earlier testimony.
mnemonic: re(다시) + cant(노래하다, 라틴어 cantare) = 다시 노래하다(=말을 바꾸다) → 철회하다. cant(노래): chant, incantation

example: The dancer wore a diaphanous veil that fluttered gracefully in the breeze.
mnemonic: dia(통하여) + phan(보이다, 그리스어 phainein) + ous = 통해 보이는 → 투명한. phan(보이다): phantom, phenomenon

example: The meaning of the ancient text remains obscure to most scholars.
mnemonic: ob(위에) + scure(덮다, 라틴어 scurus=어둠) = 어둠으로 덮다 → 모호한

example: The fetid swamp was home to countless insects and reptiles.
mnemonic: fet(냄새나다, 라틴어 fetere) + id = 냄새나는 → 악취가 나는. foetid도 같은 단어

example: She feigned illness to avoid attending the boring conference.
mnemonic: 라틴어 fingere(만들다, 꾸미다). 꾸며서 만들다 → 가장하다. fig/fict(만들다): fiction, figure, figment

example: The obsequies for the fallen hero were attended by thousands.
mnemonic: ob(향해) + sequ(따르다, 라틴어 sequi) + y = 죽은 이를 따라 보내는 → 장례식. sequ(따르다): sequence, subsequent

example: The diplomat used emollient language to ease the tension between the parties.
mnemonic: e(밖으로) + moll(부드러운, 라틴어 mollis) + ient = 부드럽게 만드는 → 완화하는. moll(부드러운): mollify

example: The scholar peregrinated through Europe studying ancient manuscripts.
mnemonic: per(통하여) + egr(들판, 라틴어 ager) + inate = 들판을 통과하다 → 여행하다. peregrine(송골매=먼 곳에서 온 새)

example: The witness prevaricated when asked direct questions about the incident.
mnemonic: pre(앞에서) + varic(다리를 벌리다, 라틴어 varicare) + ate = 다리를 벌리고 비틀거리다 → 얼버무리다. var(다양한): vary, various

example: The con artist inveigled the elderly woman into handing over her savings.
mnemonic: 프랑스어 aveugler(눈을 멀게 하다), aveugle(눈먼). 눈을 멀게 하여 → 꾀어내다, 유혹하다

example: His lifestyle is the antithesis of everything she stands for.
mnemonic: anti(반대) + thesis(놓다, 명제) = 반대에 놓인 것 → 정반대. thesis(놓다): synthesis(함께 놓다), hypothesis(아래 놓다)

example: The famous raconteur kept the dinner guests entertained with his stories.
mnemonic: 프랑스어 raconter(이야기하다). re(다시) + conter(세다, 말하다) → 이야기하는 사람

example: Her ingenuous smile charmed everyone she met.
mnemonic: in(안에) + genu(태어난, 라틴어 genus) + ous = 태어난 그대로의 → 순진한. gen(태어나다): genuine(진정한), innate

example: The insipient decision to cut funding for education had long-term consequences.
mnemonic: in(아닌) + sipi(현명한, 라틴어 sapere=맛보다, 알다) + ent = 현명하지 않은 → 어리석은. sap(알다): sapient, savant, savor

example: He made a facetious remark that lightened the mood in the meeting.
mnemonic: '퍼시셔스' → 'face(얼굴)+tious' 얼굴에 웃음을 짓는 익살스러운!

example: It would be impolitic to criticize the host at his own party.
mnemonic: im(아닌) + politic(정치적으로 현명한) = 현명치 못한. polit(시민, 현명한): politics, politic

example: The iconography of the medieval cathedral tells stories from the Bible.
mnemonic: icon(이미지, 그리스어 eikon) + graphy(기록) = 이미지의 기록 → 도상학. icon(이미지): iconic. graph(쓰다): biography

example: The gourmand sampled every dish at the lavish buffet.
mnemonic: 프랑스어 gourmand(대식가). gourmet(미식가)와 비교: gourmand는 양을, gourmet는 질을 중시

example: His bibulous habits eventually affected his health and career.
mnemonic: bib(마시다, 라틴어 bibere) + ulous = 많이 마시는 → 술을 좋아하는. bib(마시다): imbibe, beverage

example: The author's perspicuous writing style makes complex topics accessible.
mnemonic: per(통하여) + spic(보다, 라틴어 specere) + uous = 통해서 잘 보이는 → 명쾌한. spec(보다): perspicacious, inspect
